title: NappsBrandProfile
description: Brand profile passed to Claude alongside metric data. Enables contextualised, sector-aware recommendations that avoid suggesting features already active.

sector.description: Primary sector. Determines which benchmark table applies.

avg_price_point.description: Rough price positioning. budget = <€30 AOV, mid = €30–€100, premium = €100–€300, luxury = >€300.

months_on_platform.description: How many months the brand has been live on NAPPS. Affects expectation-setting — brands in month 1–3 may have lower baselines.

active_napps_features.description: List of NAPPS feature names currently active for this brand. Claude must NOT recommend features already on this list. E.g. ['Cart Abandonment Notifications', 'Loyalty Program Integration', 'Free Shipping Progress Bar'].

inactive_napps_features.description: Optional. Features explicitly available to this brand on their plan but not yet activated. Prioritise recommending these — they are zero-cost quick wins.

markets.description: Markets/countries this brand sells into. E.g. ['PT', 'ES', 'UK']. Used to contextualise localisation and market-specific features.

languages.description: Languages the app is available in. E.g. ['pt', 'es', 'en']. Informs relevance of localisation features.

has_physical_stores.description: Whether the brand has physical retail locations. Enables QR code, in-store stock, and omnichannel feature recommendations.

has_loyalty_program.description: Whether the brand has an active loyalty program (on NAPPS or elsewhere). If true and not yet integrated with NAPPS, flag as a priority.

has_subscription_products.description: Whether the brand sells subscription or recurring products. Informs Recharge recommendation relevance.

previous_recommendations.description: Top recommendations from the previous month's report. Claude should not repeat these verbatim — either escalate, report on outcome, or pivot.

notes.description: Optional free-text field for account managers to add context. E.g. 'Brand is running a major campaign in April', 'New collection launching next month', 'Recently migrated from Tapcart'. Claude should factor this into narrative framing.
